title: Bigcapital
description: Open-source double-entry accounting and financial reporting platform, a self-hosted alternative to QuickBooks, Xero and Wave.
installation: Multi-container stack. This template runs the PRIMARY backend API only: bigcapitalhq/server (listens on :3000). It is NOT usable on its own — you MUST also deploy these companion services and route them behind a reverse proxy (Envoy/Nginx): (1) FRONTEND/webapp: bigcapitalhq/webapp:latest (the SPA UI); (2) GATEWAY/proxy: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.30-latest fronting / -> webapp and /api -> server, terminating TLS on 80/443; (3) PDF rendering: gotenberg/gotenberg:7 (point GOTENBERG_URL at it, e.g. http://gotenberg:9000); (4) DB MIGRATION: a one-shot run of the same bigcapitalhq/server image with the migration command to provision the system + tenant schemas before first boot. The server multi-tenants: SYSTEM_DB_NAME is the control DB and each org gets its own DB prefixed by TENANT_DB_NAME_PERFIX. Add-ons wired: MariaDB (bigcapital-mysql:3306, app uses MySQL—not Postgres) and Valkey/Redis (rfr-bigcapital-valkey-readwrite:6379, no password) used for both cache and the BullMQ job queue. BEFORE exposing publicly ROTATE: JWT_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and the MariaDB password (default 'bigcapital'). Set BASE_URL to your public URL (pre-filled with {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}). After the stack is up, browse to the gateway URL and create the first account (no default account exists); set SIGNUP_DISABLED=true afterward to lock down registration. Optional integrations (Plaid bank sync, Stripe/Lemon Squeezy billing, S3 attachments, SMTP mail) are configured via additional env vars.
